title: mikania involucrata
intro: Mikania involucrata, also known as guaco, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, belonging to the family Asteraceae. The plant is traditionally used in folk medicine for its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which have been found to have various medicinal effects. The health benefits of Mikania involucrata include relief from symptoms of anxiety, insomnia, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
